description: Pass/fail requirements for the norm CLI: a local, encrypted screen+AX recorder with in-process Gemma-4 (mlx-vlm) reporting. Each requirement is a black-box test on CLI behavior: invocation, I/O, side effects, and exit code.

prerequisites: norm runs non-sandboxed (required to read other apps' AX trees)
macOS Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions granted to the norm/host binary
Python runtime with macapptree (capture), mlx-vlm (inference), imagehash (dedupe), SQLCipher + AES-256-GCM (storage)

exit_codes.1: runtime_error (unexpected failure during a valid operation)
exit_codes.3: auth_error (store locked, wrong passphrase, missing/denied Keychain key)
exit_codes.4: model_error (MLX weights unavailable offline, invalid model_ref, or inference failure)
exit_codes.5: not_found (no captures/preprocess output/record id in range)
exit_codes.6: environment_error (missing macOS Accessibility or Screen Recording permission, macapptree unavailable)

inference.server: none (no daemon, no socket, no HTTP endpoint started or contacted)
inference.model_ref: MLX repo id or local path
inference.weights_cache: ~/.cache/huggingface (first load downloads; later loads are local)
inference.call_pattern: load(model_ref) -> apply_chat_template(...) -> generate(model, processor, prompt, image=[...])
inference.modalities: text + image; decrypted frames passed as in-memory PIL images

config.precedence: explicit CLI flag > ~/.norm value > built-in default

defaults.prompt_interval: What did the user do over the time interval?

encryption.key_custody: random 256-bit data key in macOS Keychain (norm-scoped ACL), optionally wrapped by a norm passphrase via Argon2id

requirements.title: Version flag
pass_if: stdout matches a semantic version string
exit_code == 0
stderr is empty

requirements.title: Top-level help
pass_if: stdout lists commands: init, record, report, status, list, show, export, prune, config, passwd
exit_code == 0
fail_if: any documented command missing from help

requirements.title: No subcommand prints usage and fails
pass_if: usage text on stderr
exit_code == 2

requirements.title: Unknown command is a usage error
pass_if: stderr names 'frobnicate' as unknown
exit_code == 2

requirements.title: Config precedence: CLI flag overrides config file
pass_if: effective interval is 1 (CLI), not 9 (config)
exit_code == 0 on a successful single capture
fail_if: config value 9 is used instead of CLI value 1

requirements.title: Inference is in-process; no local inference server or socket
requirements.command: run `norm report preprocess` and `norm report interval` while monitoring sockets/processes
pass_if: norm opens no listening socket for inference
norm makes no connection to any local or remote model-server endpoint (e.g. an mlx_vlm.server or other localhost inference port)
inference occurs via mlx-vlm load()/generate() inside the norm process
fail_if: norm starts or connects to any local/remote inference server
an external daemon is required for inference

requirements.title: init creates config, data dir, and encryption key
pass_if: ~/.norm created as valid TOML containing all default keys
data_dir created
an encrypted SQLCipher index exists in data_dir
a norm data key exists in the Keychain
exit_code == 0
fail_if: the data key is derived from or equal to the login password
any plaintext capture/index file is written

requirements.title: init refuses to clobber an existing store without --force
pass_if: no existing data overwritten
stderr explains store exists and suggests --force
exit_code == 2

requirements.title: init with passphrase wraps the data key
pass_if: passphrase prompted on stdin (not echoed) or read from NORM_PASSPHRASE
stored key material wrapped via Argon2id with the passphrase
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: record --once stores one frame when present and changed
preconditions: Accessibility and Screen Recording permissions granted
screen content differs from the last stored capture
pass_if: exactly one new capture row written
row contains ts, active_app, phash, ax_hash, encrypted image_ref, encrypted ax_ref
image/AX blobs on disk are ciphertext (do not parse as PNG/JSON)
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: record skips capture while idle and stamps the gap
pass_if: no capture blob stored for the idle tick
next stored capture has idle_gap_s >= observed idle
exit_code == 0
fail_if: a capture is stored during idle

requirements.title: record dedupes an unchanged frame (phash + AX equality)
preconditions: phash within threshold AND ax_hash equal to last stored capture
pass_if: no new capture row
prior capture duration_s extended
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: record stores a new frame when only one of phash/AX changes
preconditions: phash within threshold but ax_hash differs (or vice versa)
pass_if: a new capture row IS stored (dedupe requires BOTH)
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: record honors --interval in the persistent loop
pass_if: capture attempts roughly every 1 minute (+/- slack)
runs in foreground until interrupted

requirements.title: record shuts down cleanly on SIGINT/SIGTERM
pass_if: in-flight writes flushed and store locked on signal
data key removed from process memory
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: record fails fast without OS permissions
preconditions: Screen Recording OR Accessibility permission not granted
pass_if: stderr names the missing permission and how to grant it
no partial capture written
exit_code == 6

requirements.title: record fails with auth error when store cannot be unlocked
preconditions: Keychain key absent and no/incorrect passphrase
pass_if: stderr indicates store could not be unlocked
exit_code == 3

requirements.title: record daemon lifecycle via launchd
requirements.command: norm record --install ; norm record --status ; norm record --stop
pass_if: --install registers a launchd user agent and starts it; exit_code == 0
--status reports running=true with a pid; exit_code == 0
--stop halts the agent; subsequent --status reports running=false; exit_code == 0

requirements.title: report with no subcommand prints usage and fails
pass_if: usage naming 'preprocess' and 'interval' on stderr
exit_code == 2

requirements.title: preprocess slides window K with stride J and writes one markdown per window
pass_if: number of preprocess rows == floor((N - K) / J) + 1 for N stored captures
each row stores markdown plus window_start, window_end, capture_ids, model, prompt_id
markdown blobs stored encrypted
exit_code == 0
fail_if: any preprocess markdown stored in plaintext

requirements.title: preprocess passes BOTH images and AX text to the in-process generate() call
preconditions: the generate() invocation is observable (verbose log or test seam)
pass_if: generate() receives in-memory image(s) for the window AND the window's AX text
the configured prompt_preprocess is included
no network socket / HTTP request to an inference server is opened
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: preprocess is idempotent unless --force
pass_if: second run creates no duplicate rows and skips covered windows
exit_code == 0
with --force, covered windows are recomputed and overwritten
fail_if: duplicate preprocess rows accumulate on re-run without --force

requirements.title: preprocess honors --from/--to capture range
preconditions: captures exist inside and outside the window
pass_if: only captures within [from,to] appear in any window
exit_code == 0
fail_if: captures outside the range are summarized

requirements.title: preprocess errors when the MLX model cannot be loaded
preconditions: model weights not cached and host offline, OR model_ref invalid
pass_if: stderr distinguishes 'weights unavailable offline' from 'invalid model_ref' where possible
no partial/empty preprocess row committed
exit_code == 4
fail_if: an empty markdown row is stored

requirements.title: preprocess errors when there are no captures
pass_if: stderr states there are no captures to process
exit_code == 5

requirements.title: interval aggregates preprocess markdown over [from,to] into one markdown
pass_if: generate() receives the preprocess markdown summaries within the interval plus prompt_interval
aggregated markdown emitted to stdout by default
exit_code == 0
fail_if: raw captures summarized instead of preprocess output

requirements.title: interval requires a time range
pass_if: stderr says --from/--to (or --last) is required
exit_code == 2
fail_if: a report is produced over an unbounded range

requirements.title: interval errors when no preprocess output covers the range
preconditions: captures exist but report preprocess not run for the range
pass_if: stderr explains preprocess must run first and suggests --auto-preprocess
exit_code == 5

requirements.title: interval --auto-preprocess runs preprocessing first
preconditions: captures exist but no preprocess output for the range
pass_if: missing preprocess windows computed, then interval report produced
exit_code == 0
fail_if: interval still fails with exit_code 5 despite --auto-preprocess

requirements.title: interval --output writes to a file instead of stdout
pass_if: ./week.md created with aggregated markdown
stdout quiet or prints only the path
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: status reports store and daemon state without decrypting blobs
pass_if: stdout shows data_dir, locked/unlocked, capture count, last capture ts, preprocess count, daemon state
counts come from the encrypted index, not from decrypting blobs
exit_code == 0
fail_if: status decrypts blob payloads to produce counts

requirements.title: list shows captures in a range; --json is machine-readable
pass_if: with --json, stdout is a valid JSON array of capture metadata (id, ts, active_app, idle_gap_s, duration_s)
without --json, output is a human table
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: show returns one capture's metadata and can export its artifacts
pass_if: metadata for <id> printed
with --export, decrypted PNG and AX JSON written under ./out
exit_code == 0

requirements.title: show errors on unknown id
pass_if: stderr indicates id not found
exit_code == 5

requirements.title: export decrypts a range of data to a target directory
pass_if: decrypted images, AX JSON, and preprocess markdown for the range written under ./dump
exit_code == 0
fail_if: exit_code == 0 when store could not be unlocked

requirements.title: prune deletes data in a range and supports --dry-run
pass_if: --dry-run lists what would be deleted and deletes nothing; exit_code == 0
without --dry-run, matching captures/blobs/preprocess removed and index updated; exit_code == 0
fail_if: blobs orphaned (removed from index but left on disk, or vice versa)

requirements.title: config get/set/path round-trips through ~/.norm
requirements.command: norm config set interval_minutes 10 ; norm config get interval_minutes ; norm config path
pass_if: set writes interval_minutes=10 to ~/.norm as valid TOML
get prints 10
path prints the absolute path to ~/.norm
exit_code == 0 for each

requirements.title: config rejects unknown keys
pass_if: stderr names the unknown key
~/.norm unchanged
exit_code == 2

requirements.title: Prompts are configurable and used at report time
requirements.command: norm config set prompt_preprocess 'Summarize the active task in one line.'
pass_if: subsequent report preprocess passes the new prompt text to generate()
exit_code == 0
fail_if: the default prompt is used instead of the configured one

requirements.title: model config selects the MLX model_ref used by mlx-vlm
pass_if: subsequent reports load the configured model_ref via mlx-vlm load()
exit_code == 0
fail_if: the default model_ref is loaded instead of the configured one

requirements.title: All on-disk capture data is ciphertext
requirements.command: inspect data_dir blob and index files directly (no norm involved)
pass_if: image blobs do not parse as PNG/JPEG
AX blobs do not parse as JSON
the index file is not a readable plaintext SQLite database
fail_if: any capture image, AX dump, or index row readable without the key

requirements.title: Login password is never read or held
pass_if: norm never prompts for, reads, or stores the macOS login password
the data key is a norm-specific Keychain item and/or passphrase-derived value, distinct from the login password
fail_if: the login password appears in norm's process memory, logs, or stored files

requirements.title: No elevated privileges required for any command
requirements.command: run init, record, report, and all data commands as a normal user
pass_if: every command completes (or fails for its documented reason) without sudo/root
no setuid helper or privileged daemon installed
fail_if: any command requires sudo to function

requirements.title: Every read/write of data requires a successful unlock
requirements.command: with key removed and no passphrase: norm record --once ; norm list ; norm report preprocess ; norm export
pass_if: each command refuses to read/write data and exits 3
no plaintext produced and no new ciphertext written
fail_if: any command reads or writes data while locked
any exit_code other than 3 on lock failure

requirements.title: passwd rotates the passphrase via key re-wrapping
preconditions: store unlockable with the old passphrase
pass_if: old passphrase verified before change
the data-key wrapping is re-derived with the new passphrase (key-wrapping, not bulk blob re-encryption unless explicitly requested)
after change, old passphrase fails (exit 3) and new passphrase unlocks (exit 0)
fail_if: the data key is exposed in plaintext on disk during rotation
old passphrase still unlocks after a successful change

requirements.title: Report-time decryption is transient and in-memory
requirements.command: run report preprocess/interval while monitoring data_dir and temp paths
pass_if: decrypted images are passed to generate() as in-memory PIL objects
no plaintext image or AX dump is written to disk during inference
any transient buffers are released after the call
fail_if: a plaintext image/AX file is written to disk (temp or otherwise) during reporting

requirements.title: Missing macapptree / capture dependency is an environment error
preconditions: macapptree or its native capture dependency unavailable
pass_if: stderr names the missing dependency
exit_code == 6
fail_if: a silent no-op capture is recorded
